hypothesis: If diverse scientific foundation models trained on atomic or structural data (including equivariant and non-equivariant MLIPs, UMA, Geom2Vec, and structure-based protein models) converge to a common representation of matter, then their last-layer structure-wide embeddings will show high pairwise CKNNA and IDCor alignment and low information imbalance—indicative of a shared low-dimensional manifold across molecules, materials, and proteins—while string-based models and out-of-distribution models will exhibit significantly weaker alignment.
